canto_title: The Encounter with Sordello and the Vision of the Limbo Valley

scenes.summary: After a polite greeting, Virgil introduces himself and explains that he lost his place in heaven due to his lack of faith.
Surprised by the arrival, Sordello honors Virgil's poetic legacy and asks if he comes from Hell and from which circle.
Virgil describes his existence in the first circle among the unbaptized and the virtuous pagans, and asks for help reaching Purgatory.
Virgil explains that while he can move freely, the approaching darkness makes climbing difficult, and suggests moving to a different area.
Sordello explains that the darkness of night would physically prevent them from climbing the slope.
Moved by the explanation, Virgil agrees to follow Sordello to the suggested place of rest.
The travelers descend into a valley filled with a breathtaking and overwhelming array of colors and lights.
The travelers witness a group of souls in the valley singing the 'Salve Regina' amidst flowers.
Virgil begins to identify the figures in the valley, starting with Emperor Rudolf, who failed to heal Italy.
Virgil identifies Ottacchero, describing his lineage and the contrast between him and his son.

scenes.scene_name: The Death of the Young Man
scenes.summary: Virgil points out a young man who died while fleeing, noting his physical distress.
Virgil identifies two figures who suffered because of the vices of the French monarchy.
Virgil describes a pair of singing kings and the potential greatness of the young king who followed them.
Virgil offers a brief, somber reflection on how rarely human probity rises above the clouds.
Virgil identifies Pier, who sings alongside another figure, bringing grief to certain regions.
Virgil notes the diminished status of a lineage compared to the great women of the past.
Virgil identifies Henry of England, a king characterized by a simple life.
The vision ends with the identification of Marquis Guillaume, whose wars cause great suffering.
